# bitbucketdc_repository_permissions (Resource)

Manages user and group permissions for a Bitbucket Data Center repository. This resource reconciles the desired set of permissions against the current state in Bitbucket, adding, updating, and revoking permissions as needed.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "bitbucketdc_repository_permissions" "api" {
  project_key     = "PLAT"
  repository_slug = "platform-api"

  group {
    name       = "platform-developers"
    permission = "REPO_WRITE"
  }

  group {
    name       = "platform-leads"
    permission = "REPO_ADMIN"
  }

  user {
    name       = "ci-bot"
    permission = "REPO_READ"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `project_key` (String) The key of the project that owns the repository.
- `repository_slug` (String) The slug of the repository to manage permissions for.

### Optional

- `group` (Block Set) A set of group permissions to grant on the repository. Groups not listed here will have their repository permissions revoked. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--group))
- `user` (Block Set) A set of user permissions to grant on the repository. Users not listed here will have their repository permissions revoked. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--user))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier in the format {project_key}/{repository_slug}.

<a id="nestedblock--group"></a>
### Nested Schema for `group`

Required:

- `name` (String) The Bitbucket group name.
- `permission` (String) Permission level: REPO_READ, REPO_WRITE, or REPO_ADMIN.


<a id="nestedblock--user"></a>
### Nested Schema for `user`

Required:

- `name` (String) The Bitbucket username (slug).
- `permission` (String) Permission level: REPO_READ, REPO_WRITE, or REPO_ADMIN.
